  "text": "The LLVM IR type system flows from abstract type definitions through context-owned storage to machine type representations used in code generation.\n\n## 1. Type Class Hierarchy\n\nThe base `Type` class is declared in `llvm/include/llvm/IR/Type.h` (sg-evals/llvm-project--a8f3c97d). It owns a reference to the `LLVMContext` and defines the `TypeID` enum covering all IR types (IntegerTyID, FunctionTyID, StructTyID, etc.). Types are immutable singletons—once created, never freed.\n\nDerived types are declared in `llvm/include/llvm/IR/DerivedTypes.h`:\n- `IntegerType : public Type` — arbitrary-width integer type, created via `IntegerType::get(LLVMContext&, unsigned NumBits)`\n- `FunctionType : public Type` — function signature type with return type and parameter types, created via `FunctionType::get(Type*, ArrayRef<Type*>, bool isVarArg)`\n- `StructType : public Type` — aggregate struct type (both literal/anonymous and named), created via `StructType::get(LLVMContext&, ArrayRef<Type*>, bool IsPacked)`\n\nThe SandboxIR layer (a safer wrapper) mirrors these in `llvm/include/llvm/SandboxIR/Type.h` and `llvm/lib/SandboxIR/Type.cpp`, delegating to the underlying IR types.\n\n## 2. Context Ownership\n\nAll types are uniqued and owned by the `LLVMContext`. The implementation lives in `llvm/lib/IR/LLVMContextImpl.h` (`LLVMContextImpl` class), which contains:\n- `DenseMap<unsigned, IntegerType *> IntegerTypes` — keyed by bit-width\n- `FunctionTypeSet FunctionTypes` — using `FunctionTypeKeyInfo` for hashing\n- `StructTypeSet AnonStructTypes` — literal/anonymous structs using `AnonStructTypeKeyInfo`\n- `StringMap<StructType *> NamedStructTypes` — named structs by name\n\nThe factory methods in `llvm/lib/IR/Type.cpp` (e.g., `IntegerType::get`, `FunctionType::get`, `StructType::get`) look up or insert into these context maps, ensuring type uniquing.\n\n## 3. IR Type to Machine Type Lowering in SelectionDAG\n\nThe lowering chain from IR types to machine types proceeds as follows:\n\n**Step 1 — EVT (Extended Value Type):** `llvm/include/llvm/CodeGen/ValueTypes.h` defines `EVT`, a union of `MVT` (simple machine value type) and an extended LLVM `Type*` pointer. The conversion from IR `Type*` to `EVT` is performed by `TargetLowering::getValueType(const DataLayout&, Type*, bool AllowUnknown)` in `llvm/include/llvm/CodeGen/TargetLowering.h`. For pointer types it maps to the native pointer MVT; for other types it calls `EVT::getEVT(Ty)`.\n\n**Step 2 — MVT (Machine Value Type):** `llvm/include/llvm/CodeGenTypes/MachineValueType.h` defines `MVT` as a simple enumeration of concrete machine types (i32, f64, v4i32, etc.). `EVT::getSimpleVT()` extracts the `MVT` from an `EVT`.\n\n**Step 3 — SelectionDAGBuilder:** In `llvm/lib/CodeGen/SelectionDAG/SelectionDAGBuilder.cpp`, the `SelectionDAGBuilder` translates LLVM IR instructions to `SDNode`s. It calls `TLI.getValueType(DAG.getDataLayout(), I.getType())` to obtain the `EVT` for each IR value before constructing DAG nodes.\n\n**Step 4 — LLT (Low-Level Type for GlobalISel):** The function `getLLTForType(Type&, DataLayout&)` in `llvm/include/llvm/CodeGen/LowLevelTypeUtils.h` / `llvm/lib/CodeGen/LowLevelTypeUtils.cpp` converts IR types to `LLT` (Low-Level Type), used by the GlobalISel infrastructure defined in `llvm/include/llvm/CodeGenTypes/LowLevelType.h`.\n\n**Step 5 — Reverse mapping:** `EVT::getTypeForEVT(LLVMContext&)` in `llvm/lib/CodeGen/ValueTypes.cpp` converts back from machine types to LLVM IR types when needed.\n\nThe `llvm/lib/CodeGen/SelectionDAG/TargetLowering.cpp` and `llvm/include/llvm/CodeGen/TargetLowering.h` form the central bridge, with `getValueType`, `getMemValueType`, and `getSimpleValueType` being the primary API for IR-to-machine-type conversion during SelectionDAG lowering."
}

  "text": "The LLVM IR type system flows from abstract type definitions through context-owned storage to machine type representations used in code generation.\n\n## 1. Type Class Hierarchy\n\nThe base `Type` class is declared in `llvm/include/llvm/IR/Type.h`. It holds a reference to the owning `LLVMContext` and defines the `TypeID` enum (IntegerTyID, FunctionTyID, StructTyID, PointerTyID, etc.). Types are immutable singletons—once created, they are never freed.\n\nDerived types are declared in `llvm/include/llvm/IR/DerivedTypes.h`:\n- `IntegerType : public Type` — arbitrary-width integer, created via `IntegerType::get(LLVMContext&, unsigned NumBits)`\n- `FunctionType : public Type` — function signature with return type and parameter list, created via `FunctionType::get(Type*, ArrayRef<Type*>, bool isVarArg)`\n- `StructType : public Type` — aggregate type (both anonymous/literal and named), created via `StructType::get(LLVMContext&, ArrayRef<Type*>, bool IsPacked)` or `StructType::create()`\n\nThe SandboxIR layer mirrors these in `llvm/include/llvm/SandboxIR/Type.h` and `llvm/lib/SandboxIR/Type.cpp`, wrapping the underlying IR types via a `Context` handle.\n\n## 2. Context Ownership\n\nAll IR types are uniqued and owned by the `LLVMContext`. The private implementation lives in `llvm/lib/IR/LLVMContextImpl.h` (`LLVMContextImpl`), which stores:\n- `DenseMap<unsigned, IntegerType *> IntegerTypes` — keyed by bit-width\n- `FunctionTypeSet FunctionTypes` — using `FunctionTypeKeyInfo` for structural hashing\n- `StructTypeSet AnonStructTypes` — anonymous/literal structs using `AnonStructTypeKeyInfo`\n- `StringMap<StructType *> NamedStructTypes` — named structs by identifier\n\nThe factory methods implemented in `llvm/lib/IR/Type.cpp` consult these maps to ensure type uniquing: `IntegerType::get` checks `IntegerTypes`, `FunctionType::get` checks `FunctionTypes`, and `StructType::get` checks `AnonStructTypes` (or inserts into `NamedStructTypes` for named structs).\n\n## 3. IR Type to Machine Type Lowering in SelectionDAG\n\nThe lowering chain from LLVM IR types to machine types proceeds as:\n\n**Step 1 — EVT (Extended Value Type):** `llvm/include/llvm/CodeGen/ValueTypes.h` defines `EVT`, a union of an `MVT` (simple/native machine value type) or an extended LLVM `Type*`. `TargetLowering::getValueType(const DataLayout&, Type*, bool AllowUnknown)` in `llvm/include/llvm/CodeGen/TargetLowering.h` maps an IR `Type*` to `EVT`—pointer types map to the native pointer MVT, integer/float/vector types map to corresponding MVTs.\n\n**Step 2 — MVT (Machine Value Type):** `llvm/include/llvm/CodeGenTypes/MachineValueType.h` defines `MVT` as an enumeration of concrete register-level types (i8, i32, f64, v4i32, etc.). `EVT::getSimpleVT()` extracts the `MVT`.\n\n**Step 3 — SelectionDAGBuilder:** `llvm/lib/CodeGen/SelectionDAG/SelectionDAGBuilder.cpp` translates each LLVM IR instruction to `SDNode`s. For each IR value it calls `TLI.getValueType(DAG.getDataLayout(), I.getType())` to obtain the `EVT` before constructing DAG nodes. Multiple EVTs may be needed per value for aggregate/multi-value types.\n\n**Step 4 — FunctionLoweringInfo:** `llvm/lib/CodeGen/SelectionDAG/FunctionLoweringInfo.cpp` pre-processes the IR function, computing value types for all IR values to guide SelectionDAG construction.\n\n**Step 5 — Reverse mapping:** `EVT::getTypeForEVT(LLVMContext&)` in `llvm/lib/CodeGen/ValueTypes.cpp` converts back from EVT to LLVM IR `Type*` when required.\n\n**Step 6 — GlobalISel LLT:** For the GlobalISel pipeline, `getLLTForType(Type&, DataLayout&)` in `llvm/lib/CodeGen/LowLevelTypeUtils.cpp` converts IR types to `LLT` (Low-Level Type), defined in `llvm/include/llvm/CodeGenTypes/LowLevelType.h`. This provides a target-independent representation used by GlobalISel instead of SelectionDAG's EVT/MVT."
}

## Scoping (Always Do This)

```

file:src/api/                        # Specific directory

Start narrow. Expand only if results are empty.

## Efficiency Rules

- Chain searches logically: search → read → references → definition
- Don't re-search for the same pattern; use results from prior calls
- Prefer `sg_keyword_search` over `sg_nls_search` when you have exact terms
- Read 2-3 related files before synthesising, rather than one at a time
- Don't read 20+ remote files without writing code — once you understand the pattern, start implementing

## If Stuck

1. Broaden the search query (synonyms, partial identifiers)

  "text": "The LLVM IR type system flows from abstract type definitions through context-owned storage to machine type representations used in code generation.\n\n## 1. Type Class Hierarchy\n\nThe base `Type` class is declared in `llvm/include/llvm/IR/Type.h` (sg-evals/llvm-project--a8f3c97d). It owns a reference to the `LLVMContext` and defines the `TypeID` enum covering all IR types (IntegerTyID, FunctionTyID, StructTyID, etc.). Types are immutable singletons—once created, never freed.\n\nDerived types are declared in `llvm/include/llvm/IR/DerivedTypes.h`:\n- `IntegerType : public Type` — arbitrary-width integer type, created via `IntegerType::get(LLVMContext&, unsigned NumBits)`\n- `FunctionType : public Type` — function signature type with return type and parameter types, created via `FunctionType::get(Type*, ArrayRef<Type*>, bool isVarArg)`\n- `StructType : public Type` — aggregate struct type (both literal/anonymous and named), created via `StructType::get(LLVMContext&, ArrayRef<Type*>, bool IsPacked)`\n\nThe SandboxIR layer (a safer wrapper) mirrors these in `llvm/include/llvm/SandboxIR/Type.h` and `llvm/lib/SandboxIR/Type.cpp`, delegating to the underlying IR types.\n\n## 2. Context Ownership\n\nAll types are uniqued and owned by the `LLVMContext`. The implementation lives in `llvm/lib/IR/LLVMContextImpl.h` (`LLVMContextImpl` class), which contains:\n- `DenseMap<unsigned, IntegerType *> IntegerTypes` — keyed by bit-width\n- `FunctionTypeSet FunctionTypes` — using `FunctionTypeKeyInfo` for hashing\n- `StructTypeSet AnonStructTypes` — literal/anonymous structs using `AnonStructTypeKeyInfo`\n- `StringMap<StructType *> NamedStructTypes` — named structs by name\n\nThe factory methods in `llvm/lib/IR/Type.cpp` (e.g., `IntegerType::get`, `FunctionType::get`, `StructType::get`) look up or insert into these context maps, ensuring type uniquing.\n\n## 3. Multiple EVTs may be needed per value for aggregate/multi-value types.\n\n**Step 4 — FunctionLoweringInfo:** `llvm/lib/CodeGen/SelectionDAG/FunctionLoweringInfo.cpp` pre-processes the IR function, computing value types for all IR values to guide SelectionDAG construction.\n\n**Step 5 — Reverse mapping:** `EVT::getTypeForEVT(LLVMContext&)` in `llvm/lib/CodeGen/ValueTypes.cpp` converts back from EVT to LLVM IR `Type*` when required.\n\n**Step 6 — GlobalISel LLT:** For the GlobalISel pipeline, `getLLTForType(Type&, DataLayout&)` in `llvm/lib/CodeGen/LowLevelTypeUtils.cpp` converts IR types to `LLT` (Low-Level Type), defined in `llvm/include/llvm/CodeGenTypes/LowLevelType.h`. This provides a target-independent representation used by GlobalISel instead of SelectionDAG's EVT/MVT."
